page: Amazon Web Services a1.2xlarge is an ARM64-based server powered by the first-generation AWS Graviton processor. It features 8 dedicated physical cores and 8 vCPUs utilizing the ARMv8 Cortex-A72 architecture running at 2.3 GHz. The system is equipped with 16.0 GB of system memory, providing a 2.0 GB per core ratio, and operates on the Nitro hypervisor. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, and features a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps.

Benchmark results indicate that the a1.2xlarge sits in the middle 50% tier for stress-ng single-core and multi-core CPU performance. However, memory and compression benchmarks show limitations. Memory bandwidth tests, such as large block read and write operations, register in the bottom 10% to 25% tiers. Similarly, single-threaded gzip compression and bzip3 decompression fall into the bottom 10% performance category. Geekbench and Passmark CPU scores also reflect weak to poor relative performance, indicating lower per-core computational throughput compared to newer architectures.

The primary tradeoff of the a1.2xlarge is its lower performance profile across memory-intensive and heavy computational workloads in exchange for a highly cost-efficient ARM-based resource profile. Lacking advanced vector extensions (AVX) and matrix extensions (AMX), it is not suited for high-performance computing or machine learning. Instead, its dedicated physical cores and hardware-assisted AES cryptography make it appropriate for scale-out workloads, lightweight containerized applications, basic web serving, and development environments optimized for the ARM64 architecture.
description: Amazon Web Services a1.2xlarge is an ARM64 virtual server featuring 8 dedicated physical cores powered by a 2.3 GHz AWS Graviton Cortex-A72 processor. Equipped with 16.0 GB of RAM, the instance provides a 1:2 vCPU-to-memory ratio and runs on the Nitro hypervisor. It lacks local storage and GPU accelerators, relying on a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps. Performance benchmarks place the server in the middle tier for raw CPU stress tests, but it exhibits weak to poor performance in memory bandwidth, data compression, and database operations. Lacking advanced instruction sets like AVX or AMX, the a1.2xlarge is best suited for lightweight scale-out applications, basic web serving, and ARM64 development environments where low-cost dedicated cores are prioritized over high single-core throughput.
og_description: Amazon Web Services a1.2xlarge is an ARM64 server with 8 dedicated Graviton cores, 16 GB RAM, and a 2 Gbps network baseline, offering a cost-efficient profile for lightweight scale-out workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services a1.2xlarge is an ARM64 server featuring 8 dedicated Graviton cores and 16 GB RAM, optimized for lightweight scale-out workloads.
tagline: An entry-level ARM64 virtual server featuring dedicated physical cores for cost-efficient scale-out workloads and development environments.
bullet_points: 8 dedicated ARM64 physical cores running at 2.3 GHz
16.0 GB of system memory with a 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio
2 Gbps baseline network bandwidth on the Nitro hypervisor
Best suited for lightweight containerized applications and ARM64 development
